content.text: As of the latest data, Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) is trading at $1,806.79. This token represents Ethereum in a wrapped form, allowing it to be used on different blockchain platforms.
Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC), a tokenized version of Bitcoin on the Ethereum blockchain, is currently priced at $82,251.77. This high valuation reflects its 1:1 peg with Bitcoin.
Solana (SOL), known for its high-speed blockchain capabilities, is currently valued at $124.77. It remains one of the prominent Layer 1 blockchain platforms.
The token ai16z is currently priced at $0.1603. While less known compared to major cryptocurrencies, it represents a lower-cost digital asset in the market.

categories.title: ElizaOS Plugin Fixes and Bug Reports

content.text: A recent update to the Groq plugin improves its reliability by refining the retry mechanism and enhancing error handling. Additionally, unnecessary commented-out code was removed to clean up the codebase.

Two significant bugs were identified in the ElizaOS Twitter plugin. First, a duplicate memory creation issue occurs when the bot is mentioned on Twitter, leading to database errors due to repeated memory ID usage. This is caused by both `handleTwitterInteractions()` and `handleTweet()` attempting to create the same memory. Second, the post interval configuration is not being read correctly, resulting in 'null-null minutes' being logged instead of the expected interval range. These issues affect mention handling and scheduled posting behavior and should be addressed separately.

categories.title: Documentation Updates and CLI Tool Instruction Improvements

content.text: A documentation update was submitted via a pull request titled 'chore: Update docs'. The PR includes a template for contributors to fill in key information such as related issues, risk assessment, background, type of change, and testing steps. The update appears to be procedural and focused on improving the structure and clarity of documentation contributions. No specific features or bug fixes were included in this PR.

An issue titled 'feat: Improving CLI tool instructions' outlines proposed enhancements to the Eliza CLI documentation. The improvements include adding a link to the official documentation site (eliza.how), referencing free models available on OpenRouter (https://openrouter.ai/models?max_price=0), and updating the CLI command from 'npx elizaos start' to either 'npx @elizaos/cli@beta start' or 'elizaos start' depending on installation method. These changes aim to make the CLI tool more accessible and user-friendly, especially during its beta phase.

A recent update to the Groq plugin improves its reliability by fixing the retry mechanism and enhancing error handling. Additionally, unnecessary commented-out code was removed to streamline the plugin.

A user reported issues with blocking and banning specific Twitter accounts from interacting with their AI agent. Despite removing the account from the .env file, blocking it on Twitter, and updating the server, the bot continues to engage with the unwanted account. The user requests a solution to prevent the bot from using outdated or misleading data and to enable it to verify information independently.

Two significant bugs were identified in the ElizaOS Twitter plugin. First, a duplicate memory creation issue occurs when the bot is mentioned on Twitter, causing database errors due to repeated memory IDs. Second, the post interval configuration is not being read correctly, resulting in 'null-null minutes' being logged instead of the expected interval. These issues affect both memory handling and posting behavior and require separate fixes.
Documentation updates were made to improve clarity and accuracy. This includes a fix for a typo in Thai text, correcting 'ไฟลได้ที่' to the grammatically correct 'ไฟล์ที่ได้'.

Enhancements to the CLI tool instructions were proposed, including linking to eliza.how for documentation, suggesting free models from OpenRouter, and updating the command to use the beta version of the CLI.

General documentation improvements were made, with a focus on reducing risk and clarifying deployment and testing steps. These changes aim to support better collaboration and easier onboarding for contributors.
